pergunta: So what does the non reactive mean for the hep a igm
gabarito:
Hep A IgM refers to a specific type of antibody called Immunoglobulin M (IgM) against the virus hepatitis A. When infected with hepatitis A, these antibodies are detectable at symptom onset and remain detectable for approximately three to six months. 
These antibodies might also be detectable in the first month after hepatitis A vaccination.
resposta_da_ia: In the context of a Hepatitis A IgM test, a result of non-reactive (or negative) means that the lab did not find a significant level of IgM antibodies in your blood at the time of the test
justificativa_do_juiz: A resposta do sistema de IA está tecnicamente correta, pois um resultado não reativo (ou negativo) para o teste de Hepatitis A IgM indica que não foram detectados níveis significativos de anticorpos IgM no sangue no momento do teste. No entanto, a resposta poderia ser mais completa se incluísse informações adicionais sobre o que isso significa em termos clínicos, como a possibilidade de que o paciente não esteja infectado com hepatite A ou que a infecção possa ter ocorrido há mais de seis meses. Além disso, a resposta não menciona a possibilidade de que o paciente possa ter sido vacinado recentemente contra a hepatite A, o que também poderia afetar o resultado do teste. Portanto, a resposta está alinhada com a conduta padrão, mas carece de detalhes sobre o manejo a longo prazo e possíveis interpretações alternativas.

pergunta: The ambien - is this considered a controlled substance?
gabarito: Ambien, also known as Zolpidem, is classified as a federally controlled substance by the Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA) due to its potential to cause dependence or addiction if abused or misused. If your healthcare provider prescribes this medication to you, it is important to consult with them about its benefits, risks, and proper use.
resposta_da_ia:
Yes, Ambien (generic name: zolpidem) is a Schedule IV controlled substance in the United States.
While it is a widely prescribed medication for insomnia, it is regulated by the DEA because it carries a potential for misuse and can lead to physical or psychological dependence.

Além disso, menciona o potencial de abuso e dependência, o que está alinhado com as diretrizes clínicas e a informação disponível sobre o medicamento. pergunta: The yeast infection is not transferable?
gabarito: Yeast is a type of fungus that can be found on various parts of the body, such as the skin, digestive system, and genital area. When there is a shift in the balance between yeast and other factors in the body, such as the chemical environment, immune system, and bacteria, it can lead to a yeast infection. Since yeast is a natural part of the body's ecosystem, infections are not considered contagious and are not referred to as such.
resposta_da_ia: yeast infections are not classified as sexually transmitted infections (STIs), it is actually a common misconception that they cannot be transferred.
justificativa_do_juiz: A resposta do sistema de IA está tecnicamente correta ao afirmar que as infecções por fermento não são classificadas como infecções sexualmente transmissíveis (ISTs), mas também introduz uma ambiguidade ao sugerir que elas podem ser transferidas, o que pode levar a confusão. A literatura médica estabelece que as infecções por fermento são resultado de um desequilíbrio na flora vaginal ou em outras partes do corpo e não são consideradas contagiosas. Portanto, a resposta do sistema de IA carece de clareza e precisão, pois não esclarece adequadamente que as infecções por fermento não são contagiosas, apenas que não são classificadas como ISTs.

pergunta: What can I do to help the ear pain?
gabarito: Ear pain can be treated with over-the-counter oral pain relievers, such as acetaminophen (Tylenol) or ibuprofen (Advil). Over-the-counter ear drops that contain pain relievers may offer short-term relief but should not be used if the eardrum has ruptured. Consult with your healthcare provider before ear drops are used. Placing warm or cold compresses on the ear, keeping the ear dry, and resting upright to reduce pressure in the middle ear may also help relieve pain. Do not insert any objects inside the ear.
resposta_da_ia: Try to sleep with the painful ear facing up rather than against the pillow. Keeping your head slightly elevated with an extra pillow can also help if the pain is caused by pressure buildup or fluid. Do Not Use Q-Tips: Avoid putting anything inside the ear canal. This can push wax deeper, irritate the eardrum, or even cause a secondary infection.
justificativa_do_juiz: A resposta do sistema de IA é parcialmente correta, pois menciona a importância de manter a cabeça ligeiramente elevada para aliviar a pressão no ouvido médio e evitar a introdução de objetos no canal auditivo, o que é uma conduta segura e recomendada. No entanto, a resposta omite a menção a medicamentos de alívio da dor, como acetaminofeno ou ibuprofeno, que são uma parte fundamental do tratamento da dor de ouvido. Além disso, não há menção a gotas otológicas ou compressas quentes ou frias, que também podem ser úteis no alívio da dor. Portanto, a resposta está alinhada com condutas seguras, mas falta detalhes importantes sobre o manejo da dor de ouvido.

pergunta: What causes genital warts?
gabarito: Anogenital warts, also called Condyloma acuminata, are a sign of HPV infection. These soft and raised lesions appear around the genital area or anus and are usually not painful, but may be bothersome in some cases. HPV is transmitted through contact with infected skin or mucosa and is commonly acquired through sexual contact. While warts are not necessary for transmission, they are highly contagious.
resposta_da_ia: Genital warts are caused by the human papillomavirus (HPV).

Portanto, a resposta está alinhada com a conduta padrão, mas carece de detalhes sobre a transmissão e os aspectos clínicos das verrugas genitais.

pergunta: What causes people to have such severe anxiety is it a mind over matter thing?
gabarito: The causes of anxiety are not fully understood but likely involve a combination of genetic, environmental, psychological, and developmental factors. Once an anxiety attack is triggered, a generalized systemic response is initialized through the body's autonomous sympathetic nervous system, known as the \"fight or flight\" response. This physical response is mediated through stress hormones like epinephrine (also known as adrenaline) and cortisol, which affect many systems in the body, including the cardiovascular, respiratory, and gastrointestinal systems, as well as the skin, muscles, and eyes.
resposta_da_ia: The idea that anxiety is simply "mind over matter" is a common misconception, but the reality is much more complex. While our thoughts play a role, severe anxiety is a biological and physiological event as much as it is a psychological one.
justificativa_do_juiz: A resposta do sistema de IA é tecnicamente correta, pois reconhece que a ansiedade não é apenas uma questão de "mente sobre a matéria" e que envolve fatores biológicos e fisiológicos. No entanto, a resposta omite detalhes importantes sobre os fatores genéticos, ambientais, psicológicos e de desenvolvimento que contribuem para a ansiedade, bem como a descrição específica do mecanismo de resposta do sistema nervoso simpático e a liberação de hormônios de estresse, como epinefrina e cortisol. Além disso, a resposta não fornece uma explicação clara sobre como esses fatores interagem para desencadear uma crise de ansiedade. Portanto, a resposta está alinhada com a conduta padrão, mas carece de detalhes importantes sobre a fisiopatologia da ansiedade.

pergunta: What does a low pulse possibly indicate?
gabarito: It is normal for an adult to have a pulse rate of 60-100 beats per minute (bpm). If the pulse rate falls below 60 bpm, it is known as bradycardia (low pulse). Bradycardia can be a normal physiological response in healthy individuals during sleep, endurance athletes, and older patients. However, it can also be a symptom of an underlying disease such as obstructive sleep apnea, myocardial infarction, infectious diseases, central nervous system conditions, hypothermia, hypothyroidism, anorexia, and hypoxia. Certain drugs like calcium channel blockers, lithium, opioids, and beta blockers can also cause bradycardia. Once the underlying cause is treated or the causing drug is stopped, the bradycardia usually resolves. In some cases, abnormally slow heart rhythm indicates bradyarrhythmia - a pathology in the heart conduction system.
resposta_da_ia: A low pulse, clinically known as bradycardia, is generally defined as a resting heart rate below 60 beats per minute (BPM).
justificativa_do_juiz: A resposta do sistema de IA é tecnicamente correta, pois define corretamente a bradicardia como uma frequência cardíaca em repouso abaixo de 60 batimentos por minuto (BPM). No entanto, a resposta é muito concisa e não fornece informações adicionais sobre as possíveis causas da bradicardia, como condições fisiológicas normais em atletas ou durante o sono, ou como sintoma de doenças subjacentes, como apneia obstrutiva do sono, infarto do miocárdio, doenças infecciosas, condições do sistema nervoso central, hipotermia, hipotireoidismo, anorexia e hipóxia. Além disso, a resposta não menciona os medicamentos que podem causar bradicardia, como bloqueadores de canais de cálcio, lítio, opioides e beta-bloqueadores. Portanto, a resposta está alinhada com a conduta padrão, mas carece de detalhes sobre o manejo a longo prazo e as possíveis causas da bradicardia.

pergunta: What does proton pump inhibitor do?
gabarito: Proton pump inhibitors (PPIs) are a class of medications that suppress the production of stomach acid. They work by inhibiting an enzyme in the cells of the stomach wall, which controls acid production. PPIs, such as omeprazole (Prilosec) and pantoprazole (Protonix), are used to treat conditions that are caused by the overproduction of stomach acid or worsened by stomach acid, such as acid reflux (gastroesophageal reflux disease), and peptic ulcer disease. They are also used in combination with certain antibiotics for the eradication of Helicobacter pylori infection, which is associated with peptic ulcers.
resposta_da_ia: A Proton Pump Inhibitor (PPI) is a type of medication specifically designed to significantly reduce the amount of acid produced by the glands in your stomach. Common brand names include Prilosec (Omeprazole), Nexium (Esomeprazole), and Prevacid (Lansoprazole).

Além disso, a menção a nomes de marca comuns, como Prilosec (Omeprazole), Nexium (Esomeprazole) e Prevacid (Lansoprazole), demonstra conhecimento sobre os medicamentos específicos dessa classe. No entanto, a resposta não fornece detalhes sobre o mecanismo de ação dos PPIs, como a inibição de uma enzima nas células da parede do estômago que controla a produção de ácido, nem menciona as condições específicas para as quais os PPIs são utilizados, como a doença do refluxo gastroesofágico e a doença úlcera péptica. Portanto, a resposta está alinhada com o padrão ouro, mas carece de detalhes importantes sobre o mecanismo de ação e as indicações clínicas dos PPIs.

pergunta: What exactly is cervical radiculopathy?
gabarito: Cervical radiculopathy is a condition affecting the nervous system that occurs when a nerve root in the spine is compressed due to a cervical spine pathology, such as a herniated or bulging disc, osteoarthritis, or spondylosis. This compression causes pain in the neck, shoulder, or arm, and may include other neurological issues, including paresthesia, muscle weakness, and impaired reflexes. The onset of symptoms can be sudden or gradual, depending on the cause of the compression. Diagnosis of cervical radiculopathy is based on clinical presentation, and imaging with MRI is the preferred test to identify the underlying cause. Treatment typically involves pain management, physiotherapy and referral to an orthopedic or neurosurgeon to address the root cause of the issue.
resposta_da_ia: Cervical radiculopathy is the medical term for a "pinched nerve" in the neck. It occurs when a nerve root leaving the spinal cord becomes compressed or inflamed.

No entanto, falta uma descrição mais detalhada sobre as possíveis causas, como discos herniados ou bulging, osteoartrite ou espondilose, além de não mencionar os sintomas específicos, como dor no pescoço, ombro ou braço, parestesia, fraqueza muscular e reflexos alterados. Além disso, a resposta não aborda a importância do diagnóstico baseado na apresentação clínica e na imagem por ressonância magnética (MRI) para identificar a causa subjacente, nem discute as opções de tratamento, incluindo manejo da dor, fisioterapia e encaminhamento a um ortopedista ou neurocirurgião. Portanto, a resposta está alinhada com o conhecimento básico, mas carece de profundidade e detalhes importantes para uma compreensão completa da condição.

pergunta: What is Valtrex?
gabarito: Valtrex is the brand name of the generic drug Valacyclovir, which is an oral anti-viral drug that works by slowing the growth and spread of viruses to help the body fight the infection. This drug is most commonly used to treat infections of herpes viruses, including herpes simplex virus and varicella zoster virus which cause diseases such as cold sores, genital herpes, shingles, and chickenpox. Valacyclovir (Valtrex) is available only with a prescription and should be taken as instructed by the doctor.
resposta_da_ia: Valtrex (generic name: valacyclovir) is an antiviral medication used to treat infections caused by certain types of viruses, specifically those in the herpes family

No entanto, a resposta omite detalhes importantes, como a necessidade de prescrição médica e a especificação das doenças que podem ser tratadas com Valacyclovir, como herpes simples, herpes genital, zóster, catapora, entre outras. pergunta: What is a neurological issue?
gabarito: A neurological issue is a problem that affects the nervous system, which includes the brain, spinal cord, and peripheral nerves. The nervous system is in charge of many of our human abilities, such as our ability to speak, move, and think. Neurological issues are conditions where those abilities are impaired. Among the common neurological issues are stroke, epilepsy, Multiple sclerosis, Alzheimer's disease, and Peripheral neuropathy.

Além disso, a resposta não fornece detalhes sobre como o sistema nervoso é responsável por habilidades humanas, como fala, movimento e pensamento. Portanto, a resposta está alinhada com a conduta padrão, mas carece de detalhes importantes sobre o impacto das questões neurológicas nas habilidades humanas e exemplos específicos de condições neurológicas comuns.

pergunta: What is an example of a topical steroid?
gabarito: Some examples of topical steroids are hydrocortisone, clobetasol, desoximetasone, and betamethasone. Topical steroids are classified according to their strength. The least potent topical steroid is hydrocortisone 0.5% - 1%, available over-the-counter. Other more potent topical steroids are only available with a prescription. Low-strength topical steroids are used for areas with thin skin, such as the eyelids and genitals, whereas high-potency steroids are helpful for thicker areas of skin, like the palms and soles. It is essential to only use a topical steroid for the instructed amount of time, as prolonged use can increase the risk of adverse effects.
resposta_da_ia: Topical steroids are medications applied directly to the skin to reduce inflammation, redness, and itching. They come in various strengths (potencies), ranging from mild over-the-counter versions to very strong prescription-only formulas.

Além disso, a resposta não fornece informações sobre a disponibilidade de esteroides tópicos de baixa potência para áreas com pele fina, como as pálpebras e os genitais, e a utilidade de esteroides de alta potência para áreas com pele mais espessa, como as palmas e as solas.

The least potent topical steroid is hydrocortisone 0.5% - 1%, available over-the-counter. Other more potent topical steroids are only available with a prescription. Low-strength topical steroids are used for areas with thin skin, such as the eyelids and genitals, whereas high-potency steroids are helpful for thicker areas of skin, like the palms and soles. It is essential to only use a topical steroid for the instructed amount of time, as prolonged use can increase the risk of adverse effects.
resposta_da_ia:
A topical steroid is a medication applied directly to the skin to reduce inflammation, redness, and itching.
 Common examples:(hydrocortisone ? mild, often over-the-counter; triamcinolone ? medium strength, prescription; clobetasol ? very strong (high potency), prescription only)

pergunta: What is augmentin?
gabarito: Amoxicillin with Clavulanic acid (Augmentin) is a prescription antibiotic drug used to treat many different infections caused by bacteria. This drug consists of Amoxicillin, which is a penicillin antibiotic that fights against bacteria, and Clavulanic acid, which is a beta-lactamase inhibitor that helps to treat and prevent certain bacteria resistance to Amoxicillin.
resposta_da_ia: Augmentin is a powerful, "broad-spectrum" antibiotic used to treat a wide variety of bacterial infections.

pergunta: What is blepharitis?
gabarito: Inflammation of the eyelid margins, known as blepharitis, is a common eye condition that is not contagious. Symptoms include itchy, swollen, red eyelids, tearing, a gritty sensation in the eyes, and flaking or crusting of the eyelashes in the morning. Bacterial or viral infections can cause blepharitis, blocked eyelid oil glands, contact with irritants or allergens, parasitic infestations, or inflammatory skin conditions. A physical eye exam, possibly with a slit lamp, is used to diagnose it. The primary treatment for blepharitis is good eyelid hygiene, which includes warm compresses, eyelid massages, and washing. Avoiding triggers and irritants is also essential. Artificial tear eye drops can be used if associated eye dryness is present. If symptoms are severe, persist, or worsen, your primary healthcare provider may prescribe antibiotics  or refer you to an ophthalmologist.
resposta_da_ia: Blepharitis is a common condition where the edges of your eyelids become inflamed, irritated, and itchy. It typically affects both eyes and occurs along the edges where the eyelashes grow.
justificativa_do_juiz: A resposta do sistema de IA é parcialmente correta, pois descreve blefarite como uma condição comum que causa inflamação, irritação e coceira nas bordas das pálpebras, afetando geralmente ambos os olhos. No entanto, omite detalhes importantes sobre os sintomas, como a presença de olhos vermelhos, lacrimejamento, sensação de areia nos olhos e descamação ou crosta nas pestanas pela manhã. Além disso, não menciona as possíveis causas, como infecções bacterianas ou virais, glândulas de óleo bloqueadas, contato com irritantes ou alérgenos, infestações parasitárias ou condições de pele inflamatórias. Também não aborda o diagnóstico com exame físico ocular, possivelmente com lâmpada de fenda, e o tratamento, que inclui higiene das pálpebras, compressas quentes, massagens e lavagem, além do uso de lágrimas artificiais se houver secura ocular associada. Portanto, a resposta do sistema de IA carece de detalhes importantes e não atende completamente aos padrões clínicos estabelecidos.

pergunta: What is cefdinir?
gabarito: Cefdinir (Omnicef) is an antibiotic that belongs to a class of drugs called cephalosporins. It is used to treat a variety of bacterial infections, such as pneumonia, sinus infections, strep throat, and ear infections. Cefdinir is only available with a prescription and is taken by mouth. Antacids containing aluminum or magnesium and iron supplements can interfere with the absorption of cefdinir. To prevent this interaction, take these medications at least 2 hours before or after cefdinir.
resposta_da_ia: Cefdinir (brand name Omnicef) is a third-generation cephalosporin antibiotic. Like the Augmentin we discussed, it is used to treat a wide range of bacterial infections, but it belongs to a different family of drugs

Além disso, não aborda as interações medicamentosas potenciais, como a interferência com antácidos e suplementos de ferro, que são aspectos cruciais para a segurança do paciente. pergunta: What is chlorhexidine?
gabarito: Chlorhexidine is an antiseptic and disinfectant substance that is used to prevent infection. It is effective against bacteria and fungi. It is available in various formulations, such as mouthwashes, solutions, creams, swabs, lozenges, throat sprays and soaps. Over-the-counter chlorhexidine topical antiseptics are used for the sterilization of skin before surgery, oral care, wound cleaning, and as an antiseptic hand rinse. Chlorhexidine can also be prescribed as an oral rinse for the treatment of gingivitis (gum inflammation).
resposta_da_ia:
Chlorhexidine is a widely used antiseptic and disinfectant agent that has been extensively studied and proven to be effective in reducing the risk of infection and promoting wound healing. It is a topical agent that is commonly used in various medical settings, including hospitals, clinics, and homes.

Chemical Structure:
Chlorhexidine is a bisbiguanide compound, specifically a 1,3-di-2-chlorophenylbiguanide. Its chemical structure is C12H12Cl2N4, with a molecular weight of 322.14 g/mol.

Mechanism of Action:
Chlorhexidine works by disrupting the cell membrane of microorganisms, ultimately leading to their death. It has a broad-spectrum antimicrobial activity, effective against a wide range of bacteria, viruses, fungi, and protozoa. The exact mechanism of action involves:

1. Disruption of the cell membrane: Chlorhexidine binds to the phospholipid bilayer of the cell membrane, causing it to become permeable and leading to the loss of essential ions and nutrients.
2. Inhibition of protein synthesis: Chlorhexidine has been shown to inhibit protein synthesis in microorganisms, which is essential for their survival and growth.
3. Denaturation of proteins: Chlorhexidine can denature proteins, making them unable to perform their normal functions.

Uses:
Chlorhexidine is commonly used in various medical settings, including:

1. Skin antisepsis: Chlorhexidine is used to clean and disinfect the skin before surgical procedures, injections, and other invasive medical procedures.
2. Wound care: Chlorhexidine is used to treat and prevent wound infections, including burns, ulcers, and surgical wounds.
3. Oral care: Chlorhexidine is used as a mouthwash to reduce plaque, gingivitis, and bad breath.
4. Disinfection of surfaces: Chlorhexidine is used to disinfect surfaces, equipment, and medical devices

No entanto, falta uma menção explícita sobre a disponibilidade de formulações específicas, como enxaguantes bucais, soluções, cremes, swabs, pastilhas, sprays de garganta e sabonetes, que são mencionadas no padrão ouro. Portanto, a resposta é correta, mas carece de alguns detalhes importantes para uma avaliação completa.

pergunta: What is sciatica
gabarito: Sciatica refers to pain that travels along the sciatic nerve path due to sciatic nerve injury or irritation. The most common cause of sciatica is compression of the sciatic nerve root by a herniated disc in the spine. Classically, the pain affects one side of the body and radiates from the buttock down the back of the leg to below the knee. The pain can be described as burning or stabbing and may be accompanied by paresthesia (tingling), numbness, or weakness. Symptoms may worsen with twisting, bending, sneezing, or coughing. It is typically diagnosed based on the characteristic pain and a physical examination. Diagnostic imaging studies may be recommended if symptoms persist for >6 weeks or weakness or numbness is experienced. The risk of developing sciatica can be reduced by maintaining good posture and healthy weight, regular exercise, muscle strengthening exercises, and proper lifting techniques. Mild sciatica usually resolves in a few weeks. The following conservative approaches may help relieve pain and speed up recovery: cold or heat packs, over-the-counter analgesics, physiotherapy, and starting gentle exercise as soon as tolerated. Contact your healthcare advisor if symptoms do not improve after a few weeks, worsen, or stop you from doing your normal activities. Seek immediate medical attention for severe pain, numbness, or weakness in both legs or problems controlling bowel or bladder function.
resposta_da_ia:
Sciatica is a common and often misunderstood term that refers to a set of symptoms, rather than a specific diagnosis. It is characterized by pain, numbness, tingling, or weakness in the lower back, hips, and legs, typically affecting one side of the body.

The sciatic nerve is the longest nerve in the body, extending from the lower back down to the legs. It is formed by the combination of the L4, L5, and S1 spinal nerve roots, which exit the spinal canal and merge to form the sciatic nerve. The sciatic nerve is responsible for controlling the muscles of the lower leg, as well as providing sensation to the skin of the lower back, hips, and legs.

Sciatica can occur when there is compression, irritation, or inflammation of the sciatic nerve, which can be caused by a variety of factors, including:

1. Herniated discs: When a disc in the lower back bulges or herniates, it can put pressure on the sciatic nerve, causing pain and discomfort.
2. Degenerative disc disease: As we age, the discs in our spine can degenerate, leading to compression of the sciatic nerve.
3. Spinal stenosis: Narrowing of the spinal canal can put pressure on the sciatic nerve, causing symptoms.
4. Piriformis syndrome: Compression of the sciatic nerve by the piriformis muscle can cause sciatica-like symptoms.
5. Spondylolisthesis: A condition in which a vertebra slips out of place, putting pressure on the sciatic nerve.
6. Muscle strain: Tight or strained muscles in the lower back can irritate the sciatic nerve, causing pain and discomfort.
7. Obesity: Excess weight can put additional pressure on the sciatic nerve, contributing to sciatica symptoms.

pergunta: What is the most common cause of dysuria in young, sexually active, healthy men?
gabarito: The most common cause of dysuria in young, sexually active, healthy men is a sexually transmitted infection (STI). STIs can result in inflammation of the urethra (urethritis), epididymis (epididymitis), and prostate (prostatitis). These conditions are associated with dysuria and an urgent, frequent need to urinate. Signs and symptoms of urethritis may also include pain during sexual intercourse and itching at the tip of the penis. Epididymitis usually presents with a swollen, painful scrotum, and prostatitis is associated with pain in the perineum. Discharge from the penis can be a sign of an STI. The most common STIs are gonorrhoea and chlamydia. Diagnostic evaluation typically includes a physical examination, medical and sexual history taking, and urine tests such as urinalysis, urine culture, and urethral swabs.

The high prevalence of CT and GC infections in young, sexually active men is likely due to the increased risk of sexual transmission and the lack of symptoms in many cases. CT and GC infections can cause urethritis, which is characterized by dysuria, urethral discharge, and sometimes, hematuria.

It is essential to note that other causes of dysuria, such as urinary tract infections (UTIs), prostatitis, and epididymitis, should also be considered in the differential diagnosis. However, based on the available evidence, CT and GC infections are the most common causes of dysuria in young, sexually active, healthy men.

In conclusion, the most common cause of dysuria in young, sexually active, healthy men is likely to be urethritis caused by Chlamydia trachomatis or Neisseria gonorrhoeae infections.

pergunta: What should do if I get a fever?

Treating a fever can help alleviate accompanying symptoms like headaches, muscle and joint pain, or febrile seizures in children. Medications like acetaminophen (paracetamol) or non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) such as ibuprofen can be used to lower fever. Acetaminophen is usually recommended first due to fewer side effects than NSAIDs, but short-term use of either drug is safe for healthy individuals without kidney or liver impairment. A fever is a common symptom that can occur in people of all ages. As a medical professional, I'll provide you with evidence-based guidance on what to do if you get a fever.

**What is a fever?**

A fever is a body temperature above the normal range of 98.6°F (37°C). In adults, a fever is typically defined as a temperature of 100.4°F (38°C) or higher, while in children, it's often considered a fever if their temperature is 100.4°F (38°C) or higher.

**What causes a fever?**

Fever is a natural response of the body to infection, inflammation, or other underlying conditions. It's a way for the body to fight off pathogens and promote healing. Common causes of fever include:

1. Infections (e.g., respiratory, urinary, or skin infections)
2. Inflammatory conditions (e.g., arthritis, gout, or inflammatory bowel disease)
3. Viral infections (e.g., flu, common cold, or viral gastroenteritis)
4. Bacterial infections (e.g., pneumonia, meningitis, or sepsis)
5. Medications (e.g., antibiotics, antihistamines, or certain antidepressants)

**What to do if you get a fever:**

1. **Monitor your temperature**: Use a thermometer to check your temperature regularly. You can use a digital thermometer or a mercury thermometer.
2. **Stay hydrated**: Drink plenty of fluids, such as water, clear broth, or electrolyte-rich beverages like sports drinks. Aim for at least 8-10 glasses of fluid per day.
3. **Rest**: Fever can make you feel tired and weak. Rest and avoid strenuous activities to help your body recover.
4. **Take over-the-counter medications**: Acetaminophen (Tylenol) or ibuprofen (Advil, Motrin) can help reduce fever and alleviate symptoms like headache

pergunta: Whats the difference between Valtrex and Acyclovir?
gabarito: Valacyclovir (Valtrex) and acyclovir (Zovirax, Sitavig) are both antiviral drugs prescribed to treat the infections caused by herpes simplex virus, and varicella-zoster virus, which include cold sores, genital herpes, chicken pox, and shingles. Acyclovir is also used to treat herpes in neonates and herpes simplex encephalitis. Molecularly speaking, Valacyclovir is a prodrug of acyclovir, which is converted into acyclovir in the body. Among other differences is that Valacyclovir is only available as oral tablets, whereas acyclovir is available as oral tablets, oral capsules, sublingual tablets, oral suspensions, creams, and injectable solutions. In addition, Valacyclovir has a longer duration of action and greater bioavailability compared to acyclovir. Therefore, an advantage of prescribing Valacyclovir is that it can be taken fewer times in a day compared to acyclovir and still has the same effect.

Valtrex (valacyclovir) and Acyclovir are both antiviral medications used to treat herpes simplex virus (HSV) infections, including cold sores, genital herpes, and shingles. While they share some similarities, there are key differences between the two medications.

Similarities:

1. Mechanism of action: Both Valtrex and Acyclovir work by inhibiting the replication of HSV, thereby reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.
2. Antiviral activity: Both medications have antiviral activity against HSV-1 and HSV-2, the viruses that cause cold sores and genital herpes.

pergunta: Which doctor should i go to for vertigo
gabarito: Vertigo is a symptom of an illusory movement that some people may describe as dizziness. If you are experiencing vertigo, it is essential to determine the type and cause of your symptoms. You can consult with your general physician, who will evaluate your symptoms and perform a physical examination. If necessary, they may refer you to an otolaryngologist or a neurologist for further testing and treatment. It is crucial to seek immediate medical attention if you experience vertigo or dizziness with any of the following symptoms: constant dizziness that doesn't go away and isn't related to head movement, difficulty speaking or understanding others speaking, weakness of face or extremities, confusion, slurred speech, inability to stand, sudden and severe headache or neck pain, difficulty swallowing, or double vision.
resposta_da_ia:
Vertigo is a complex condition that can be caused by a variety of factors, including inner ear problems, neurological disorders, and other underlying medical conditions. As a result, it's often best to see a specialist who has experience in diagnosing and treating vertigo. Here are some options to consider:

1. Ear, Nose, and Throat (ENT) Specialist: ENT specialists, also known as otolaryngologists, are trained to diagnose and treat ear-related conditions, including vertigo. They can perform tests such as the Dix-Hallpike maneuver and electronystagmography (ENG) to help identify the underlying cause of your vertigo.
2. Neurologist: Neurologists are trained to diagnose and treat conditions affecting the brain and nervous system, including vertigo. They can perform tests such as the Romberg test and the head impulse test to help identify the underlying cause of your vertigo.
3. Physical Therapist: Physical therapists who specialize in vestibular rehabilitation therapy (VRT) can help you manage your vertigo symptoms and improve your balance and coordination. They can also provide exercises and stretches to help alleviate your symptoms.
4. Chiropractor: Chiropractors who specialize in treating vertigo can use spinal manipulation and other techniques to help alleviate your symptoms. However, it's important to note that the effectiveness of chiropractic care for vertigo is still debated and more research is needed to fully understand its benefits.

When choosing a doctor to see for vertigo, consider the following factors:

* Experience: Look for a doctor who has experience in diagnosing and treating vertigo. Ask about their experience and success rates in treating patients with vertigo.
* Specialization: Consider seeing a specialist who has expertise in treating vertigo, such as an ENT specialist or a neurologist.
* Availability: Make sure the doctor has availability to see you at a time that is convenient for you.
* Insurance: Check with your insurance provider to see if they cover the doctor

treatment of cervical cancer 5 years ago. She reports constant,
severe aching pain; she rates the pain at its maximum as a 10 on a 10?point
scale. The pain worsens during intercourse. All
subsequent cancer follow-up has been negative. Medical history otherwise
is unremarkable. The patient has taken oxycodone for
her pain for 3 years, but she now reports that the oxycodone only partially
relieves her pain. She requests either an increased dose of
oxycodone or a change in her prescription to controlled-release morphine,
which was recently given to her by a friend. She has
smoked one pack of cigarettes daily for the past 15 years. She does not
drink alcoholic beverages. BMI is 18 kg/m2. She appears

Urine toxicology screening is positive for diazepam, methadone, and

to discuss these results. Which of the following is the most appropriate
opening remark?
(A) "I am concerned that you are abusing pain medicine. I would like for you
to consider a substance use disorder
treatment program."
(B) "I cannot be your physician any longer because of your drug use
disorder. I'm going to give you 30 days to find a new
physician."
(C) "I would like to know where you have been getting the methadone and

(D) "The test showed that you take other drugs that I have not prescribed

justificativa_do_juiz: A pergunta apresentada refere-se a uma paciente com dor pélvica crônica pós-histerectomia e radioterapia devido a câncer de colo uterino. Ela está tomando oxycodone há 3 anos, mas relata que o medicamento não alivia mais sua dor e solicita aumento da dose ou mudança para morfina de liberação controlada. No entanto, o exame toxicológico de urina revela a presença de diazepam, metadona e oxycodone, indicando uso de substâncias não prescritas. A resposta do AI, "C", questiona a paciente sobre a fonte das substâncias não prescritas, o que pode ser visto como uma abordagem investigativa, mas não necessariamente a mais apropriada para abordar o problema do uso de substâncias. A resposta correta, de acordo com o padrão ouro, é a opção "A", que expressa preocupação com o uso abusivo de medicamentos para dor e sugere considerar um programa de tratamento para distúrbios do uso de substâncias. Essa abordagem é mais empática e direcionada para o tratamento do problema subjacente, em vez de simplesmente questionar a fonte das substâncias ou ameaçar encerrar o atendimento médico. Portanto, a resposta do AI não é a mais apropriada, pois não prioriza a saúde e o bem-estar da paciente de forma holística.

pergunta:
Question 51.1 (MC-NJ) - Step 3 A 24-year-old woman, gravida 1, para 1, comes to the office because she and
her husband have been trying to conceive for
the past year without success. She gave birth to her first child at age 18
years by cesarean delivery following fetal distress.
She then developed postpartum endometritis that resolved with
intravenous antibiotic therapy. She used an intrauterine
device (IUD) for 1 year following her first pregnancy but had the device
removed because of intermenstrual bleeding.
Medical history is also remarkable for chlamydial cervicitis treated with
antibiotic therapy as an outpatient at age 21 years,
and recurrent bacterial vaginosis that had been treated with metronidazole
therapy during her pregnancy and on two other
occasions. Vital signs today are within normal limits. Breast and pelvic
examinations disclose no abnormalities.
Hysterosalpingography shows bilateral hydrosalpinx. Which of the
following factors in this patient's history most increased
her risk for development of her condition?
(A) Bacterial vaginosis
(B) Cesarean delivery
(C) Chlamydial cervicitis
(D) Postpartum endometritis
(E) Use of an IUD
justificativa_do_juiz: A paciente apresenta histerossalpingografia com bilateral hidrosalpinx, o que sugere uma obstrução das trompas de Falópio. A história de chlamydial cervicitis tratada com antibioticoterapia é um fator de risco importante para o desenvolvimento de doença inflamatória pélvica (DIP) e subsequente obstrução das trompas de Falópio. A chlamydial cervicitis é uma causa comum de DIP, que pode levar à formação de aderências e obstrução das trompas de Falópio, resultando em infertilidade. Embora a paciente também tenha histórico de cesárea, endometrite pós-parto, vaginose bacteriana e uso de DIU, esses fatores não estão diretamente relacionados ao desenvolvimento de hidrosalpinx. Portanto, a resposta correta é a chlamydial cervicitis, que é o fator que mais aumentou o risco de desenvolver a condição. No entanto, a resposta fornecida pelo sistema de IA é a endometrite pós-parto, que não é o fator mais relevante para o desenvolvimento de hidrosalpinx.

justificativa_do_juiz: A paciente apresenta bilateral hidrosalpinx, uma condição caracterizada pela dilatação das trompas de Falópio devido à obstrução. A cesárea (opção B) é um fator de risco para várias complicações, mas não está diretamente relacionada à obstrução das trompas de Falópio. Portanto, a resposta correta é a opção C, Chlamydial cervicitis, que é a causa mais provável do desenvolvimento de hidrosalpinx nessa paciente.

worse."
History of Present Illness: 3-month history of worsening leg pain, pain
exacerbated by walking; peak intensity after 1 block, pain resolves
completely with rest, pain rated 4/10 at worst
Past Medical History: hypertension, mild angina, type 2 diabetes mellitus

Allergies: no known drug allergies

years

BP 140/90 mmHg

Weight 90 kg (198 lb)
BMI 33 kg/m²
Appearance: no acute distress
HEENT: funduscopic shows grade 2/4 arteriovenous nicking
Neck: no jugular venous distention

hepatosplenomegaly

Question: Which of the following is the most appropriate diagnostic study?
(A) Ankle brachial index
(B) Arteriography
(C) ECG
(D) Echocardiography
(E) MUGA scan
justificativa_do_juiz: O paciente apresenta sintomas de claudicação intermitente, caracterizada por dor nas pernas ao caminhar que melhora com repouso. Essa condição é frequentemente associada à doença arterial periférica (DAP). O índice tornozelo-braquial (ITB) é um exame não invasivo e amplamente utilizado para diagnosticar e monitorar a DAP. Ele fornece uma medida da pressão arterial nos membros inferiores em relação aos membros superiores, ajudando a identificar a presença e a gravidade da doença arterial periférica. Considerando a história clínica do paciente, com dor nas pernas ao caminhar e fatores de risco como tabagismo de longa duração, hipertensão, diabetes mellitus tipo 2 e dislipidemia (sugestiva pelo uso de lovastatina), o ITB é o exame mais apropriado para iniciar a investigação diagnóstica. Além disso, o ITB é uma recomendação das diretrizes clínicas para o diagnóstico inicial da DAP devido à sua acessibilidade, baixo custo e não invasividade.

pergunta:
Question 53.1 (MC-NJ) - Step 3 In determining the usefulness of a D-dimer assay in ruling out pulmonary
embolism as a diagnosis in a 27-year-old woman
with pleuritic chest pain and nonproductive cough, a recent study in the
medical literature is reviewed. The study evaluates
the use of D-dimer assay in 1500 patients (705 female and 795 male)
ranging in age from 19 years to 57 years (mean age is
36 years), whose examining physicians had a low clinical suspicion that the
patients had pulmonary embolism. All patients in
the study underwent a D-dimer assay, followed by CT angiography of the
chest to confirm or exclude the diagnosis of
pulmonary embolism. Study results are shown:
D-Dimer Positive and CT Angiography Positive: 88
D-Dimer Negative and CT Angiography Positive: 0
D-Dimer Positive and CT Angiography Negative: 312
D-Dimer Negative and CT Angiography Negative: 1100
Total CT Angiography Positive: 88
Total CT Angiography Negative: 1412
Total D-Dimer Positive: 400
Total D-Dimer Negative: 1100
Which of the following is the most appropriate conclusion about this data
in regard to patients with low clinical suspicion for
pulmonary embolism?
(A) D-Dimer assay results should be used to determine the need for
ordering CT angiography for patients suspected of
having a pulmonary embolism
(B) False-negative D-dimer assay results are common in patients with
confirmed pulmonary embolism
(C) False-positive D-dimer assay results are rarely encountered
(D) A negative D-dimer assay has a high negative predictive value for
excluding the diagnosis of pulmonary embolism
(E) A positive D-Dimer assay has a high positive predictive value for
diagnosing pulmonary embolism
justificativa_do_juiz: A resposta fornecida pelo sistema de IA é "D", que afirma que um resultado negativo do teste de D-dímero tem um alto valor preditivo negativo para excluir o diagnóstico de embolia pulmonar. Isso está alinhado com as diretrizes clínicas atuais, pois o teste de D-dímero é amplamente utilizado como uma ferramenta de triagem para pacientes com baixa suspeita clínica de embolia pulmonar. Os resultados do estudo apresentado mostram que não houve casos de D-dímero negativo com angiografia por tomografia computadorizada (CT) positiva, o que sugere que um resultado negativo do teste de D-dímero pode ser confiável para excluir o diagnóstico de embolia pulmonar em pacientes com baixa suspeita clínica. Além disso, o alto valor preditivo negativo do teste de D-dímero é consistente com as recomendações da American Heart Association (AHA) e da Sociedade Brasileira de Cardiologia (SBC) para o diagnóstico e tratamento da embolia pulmonar.

Os dados fornecidos mostram que não houve resultados falsos negativos para o exame de D-dimer (D-Dimer Negative and CT Angiography Positive: 0), o que indica que um resultado negativo para D-dimer tem um alto valor preditivo negativo para excluir a diagnose de embolia pulmonar. Isso é consistente com a prática clínica, onde um resultado negativo de D-dimer em pacientes com baixa probabilidade de embolia pulmonar pode ser usado para descartar a doença, evitando assim a necessidade de exames de imagem adicionais, como a angiografia por tomografia computadorizada. Portanto, a resposta D é a mais apropriada, pois reflete a utilidade do exame de D-dimer na exclusão de embolia pulmonar em pacientes com baixa suspeita clínica.

pergunta:
Question 53.1 (MC-NJ) - Step 3 In determining the usefulness of a D-dimer assay in ruling out pulmonary
embolism as a diagnosis in a 27-year-old woman
with pleuritic chest pain and nonproductive cough, a recent study in the
medical literature is reviewed. The study evaluates
the use of D-dimer assay in 1500 patients (705 female and 795 male)
ranging in age from 19 years to 57 years (mean age is
36 years), whose examining physicians had a low clinical suspicion that the
patients had pulmonary embolism. All patients in
the study underwent a D-dimer assay, followed by CT angiography of the
chest to confirm or exclude the diagnosis of
pulmonary embolism. Study results are shown:
D-Dimer Positive and CT Angiography Positive: 88
D-Dimer Negative and CT Angiography Positive: 0
D-Dimer Positive and CT Angiography Negative: 312
D-Dimer Negative and CT Angiography Negative: 1100
Total CT Angiography Positive: 88
Total CT Angiography Negative: 1412
Total D-Dimer Positive: 400
Total D-Dimer Negative: 1100
Which of the following is the most appropriate conclusion about this data
in regard to patients with low clinical suspicion for
pulmonary embolism?
(A) D-Dimer assay results should be used to determine the need for
ordering CT angiography for patients suspected of
having a pulmonary embolism
(B) False-negative D-dimer assay results are common in patients with
confirmed pulmonary embolism
(C) False-positive D-dimer assay results are rarely encountered
(D) A negative D-dimer assay has a high negative predictive value for
excluding the diagnosis of pulmonary embolism
(E) A positive D-Dimer assay has a high positive predictive value for
diagnosing pulmonary embolism
justificativa_do_juiz: A resposta do sistema de IA é incorreta porque um resultado positivo no teste de D-dímero não tem um alto valor preditivo positivo para diagnosticar embolia pulmonar. Isso ocorre porque o número de resultados falsos-positivos (312) é significativamente alto em comparação com os resultados verdadeiros-positivos (88). Além disso, a presença de 312 resultados falsos-positivos indica que o teste de D-dímero pode não ser específico o suficiente para confirmar o diagnóstico de embolia pulmonar. Por outro lado, a ausência de resultados falsos-negativos (0) e o grande número de resultados verdadeiros-negativos (1100) sugerem que um resultado negativo no teste de D-dímero tem um alto valor preditivo negativo para excluir o diagnóstico de embolia pulmonar, o que está alinhado com a opção D. Portanto, a conclusão mais apropriada é que um resultado negativo no teste de D-dímero tem um alto valor preditivo negativo para excluir o diagnóstico de embolia pulmonar, e não que um resultado positivo tenha um alto valor preditivo positivo para diagnosticar embolia pulmonar.

pergunta:
Question 54.1 (MC-NJ) - Step 3 A 45-year-old man is admitted to the hospital because of a 6-hour history
of bilateral vision loss. He is otherwise
asymptomatic. He has no history of serious illness and takes no
medications. Vital signs are within normal limits. Physical
examination findings are consistent with psychogenic blindness. A medical
student on the patient's treatment team suggests
to the attending physician that they administer a placebo 0.9% saline
injection to the patient but tell the patient it is a new
medication that will "confirm a diagnosis of blindness by temporarily
recovering your vision." Which of the following best
describes the appropriateness of the medical student's suggestion?
(A) It is appropriate because it can prove that the patient is not blind
(B) It is appropriate because the medical student's intent is to help the
patient
(C) It is inappropriate because it is invasive and could be painful
(D) It is inappropriate because the physician would not be stating the truth
(E) It is inappropriate without the approval of the hospital ethics
committee
justificativa_do_juiz: A sugestão do estudante de medicina de administrar uma injeção de solução salina a 0,9% como um placebo, informando ao paciente que se trata de um novo medicamento que pode "confirmar o diagnóstico de cegueira temporariamente restaurando a visão", é inapropriada porque envolve uma falta de transparência e honestidade com o paciente. Isso viola os princípios éticos da medicina, que incluem a obrigação de informar os pacientes sobre seu tratamento de maneira clara e honesta. A administração de um placebo sem o conhecimento ou consentimento do paciente pode ser considerada enganosa e não respeita a autonomia do paciente. Além disso, a relação médico-paciente se baseia na confiança, e tal prática poderia comprometer essa confiança. Portanto, a resposta correta é a opção D, pois a sugestão do estudante de medicina é inapropriada porque o médico não estaria dizendo a verdade ao paciente.

pergunta:
Question 55.1 (MC-NJ) - Step 3 A 5-week-old infant is brought to the office by his mother for an initial
well-child visit. He was born at 37 weeks' gestation
via cesarean delivery because of chorioamnionitis. The infant had fetal
distress that was documented by fetal monitoring
during labor. The mother became jaundiced in the immediate postpartum
period and was found to have hepatitis C (HCV)
and HIV infection. She is unaware of her hepatitis B (HBV) status but knows
that her son received the hepatitis B
vaccination on the second day of life. The infant's weight today is 3912 g (8
lb 10 oz); physical examination shows no
abnormalities. The mother wants to know what chance her child has of
having contracted HIV or HCV infection from her.
The risk for vertical transmission of HIV is approximately 20%. The mother
is advised and appropriate management is
planned for the infant regarding HIV. To determine the risk of HCV
transmission, a recently published study of risk factors
for perinatal transmission of HCV in 155 mothers coinfected with HIV and
HCV is reviewed. Results of the study are shown:
Association of Gestational and Infant Factors With Mother-to-Infant HCV
Transmission
155 Mothers with HIV and HCV Infection
Factor: Gestational Age <37 weeks - HCV Transmission 8.4%, RR 1.0, 95% CI
-
Factor: Gestational Age ?37 weeks - HCV Transmission 8.3%, RR 0.99, 95%
CI 0.32 to 3.06, p Value 0.99
Factor: No Cesarean Delivery - HCV Transmission 6.0%, RR 1.0, 95% CI -
Factor: With Cesarean Delivery - HCV Transmission 13.3%, RR 2.21, 95% CI
0.69 to 7.06, p Value0.24
Factor: No Chorioamnionitis - HCV Transmission 7.0%, RR 1.0, 95% CI -
Factor: With Chorioamnionitis - HCV Transmission 33.3%, RR 4.77, 95% CI
0.86 to 26.3, p Value 0.21
Factor: Did not use Fetal Electrode - HCV Transmission 7.0%, RR 1.0, 95%
CI 
Factor: Used of Fetal Electrode - HCV Transmission 3.3%, RR 0.66, 95% CI
0.09 to 4.89, p Value 0.99
Factor: Infant Not HIV-infected - HCV Transmission 5.4%, RR 1.0 95% CI 
Factor: Infant HIV-infected - HCV Transmission 17.1%, RR 3.19, 95% CI 1.14
to 8.93, p Value 0.04
RR=relative risk; CI=confidence interval
Based on these data, which of the following is the most appropriate
conclusion regarding the five potential risk factors for
transmission of HCV?
(A) All factors appeared to have an effect on HCV transmission
(B) Fetal electrode monitoring was protective against HCV
(C) Infant HIV infection was the only significantly associated factor
(D) Maternal chorioamnionitis was the most significantly associated factor
(E) No factor was significantly associated
justificativa_do_juiz: A análise dos dados apresentados no estudo sobre a transmissão vertical de HCV em mães coinfetadas com HIV e HCV permite concluir que, entre os fatores avaliados, apenas a infecção por HIV do bebê apresentou uma associação significativa com a transmissão de HCV, com um risco relativo (RR) de 3,19 e um intervalo de confiança (IC) de 1,14 a 8,93, resultando em um p-valor de 0,04. Isso sugere que a infecção por HIV no recém-nascido é um fator de risco significativo para a transmissão de HCV. Embora outros fatores, como a presença de corioamnionite, tenham apresentado taxas de transmissão mais altas, os resultados não foram estatisticamente significativos devido aos grandes intervalos de confiança e p-valores maiores que 0,05. Portanto, a conclusão mais apropriada com base nos dados fornecidos é que a infecção por HIV no bebê é o único fator significativamente associado à transmissão de HCV.

pergunta:
Question 56.1 (MC-NJ) - Step 3 A 45-year-old man comes to the community health center because of a 1-
week history of fever, chills, sweating, mild
shortness of breath, and nonproductive cough. Medical history is
remarkable for AIDS and pneumonia 3 years ago that
required hospitalization. Today, he says that he lost his job 6 months ago
and cannot afford to buy his antiretroviral
medications. Vital signs are temperature 38.6°C (101.5°F), pulse 82/min,
respirations 20/min, and blood pressure
116/72 mmHg. Pulse oximetry on room air shows an oxygen saturation of
97%. The health center is very busy with several
patients waiting to be evaluated. Which of the following is the most
appropriate next step?
(A) Arrange a direct admission to the hospital for this patient
(B) Continue with obtaining a thorough history and examining this patient
(C) Place masks on the patient and yourself and then continue to evaluate
him
(D) Send this patient for chest x-rays, and while they are being obtained
examine the next patient
justificativa_do_juiz: O paciente em questão apresenta sintomas de infecção respiratória, como febre, calafrios, suor, falta de ar leve e tosse não produtiva, além de ter um histórico de AIDS e pneumonia prévia. Considerando o contexto de uma pessoa com sistema imunológico comprometido devido à AIDS e o fato de não estar aderindo ao tratamento antirretroviral devido a questões financeiras, é fundamental tomar medidas para prevenir a transmissão de possíveis doenças infecciosas. A opção C, que sugere colocar máscaras no paciente e no próprio profissional de saúde antes de continuar a avaliação, é a conduta mais apropriada como próximo passo. Isso porque a colocação de máscaras é uma medida de isolamento respiratório que ajuda a prevenir a transmissão de agentes patógenos transmitidos por via aérea, o que é especialmente importante em pacientes imunocomprometidos. Além disso, essa medida é rápida, simples e não atrasa significativamente a avaliação do paciente, podendo ser implementada mesmo em um contexto de grande demanda, como o descrito.

respirations 20/min, and blood pressure
116/72 mmHg. Pulse oximetry on room air shows an oxygen saturation of
97%. The health center is very busy with several
patients waiting to be evaluated. Which of the following is the most
appropriate next step?
(A) Arrange a direct admission to the hospital for this patient
(B) Continue with obtaining a thorough history and examining this patient
(C) Place masks on the patient and yourself and then continue to evaluate
him

examine the next patient
justificativa_do_juiz: O paciente em questão apresenta sintomas de infecção respiratória, como febre, calafrios, suor, falta de ar leve e tosse não produtiva, e tem um histórico de AIDS e pneumonia prévia. Além disso, ele não está mais tomando seus medicamentos antirretrovirais devido a problemas financeiros. Considerando esses fatores, é fundamental priorizar a prevenção de transmissão de doenças infecciosas, especialmente em um ambiente de saúde com vários pacientes esperando para ser avaliados. A opção C, que envolve colocar máscaras no paciente e no profissional de saúde antes de continuar a avaliação, é a conduta mais apropriada para minimizar o risco de transmissão de doenças. A opção A, que sugere admissão direta ao hospital, pode ser prematura sem uma avaliação mais completa. A opção B, que continua com a obtenção de história e exame físico sem medidas de prevenção, não aborda o risco de transmissão. A opção D, que envia o paciente para radiografias de tórax enquanto atende o próximo paciente, também não prioriza a prevenção da transmissão de doenças. Portanto, a resposta do sistema de IA, que sugere admissão direta ao hospital (opção A), não é a conduta mais apropriada como primeiro passo.

pergunta:
Question 57.1 (MC-NJ) - Step 3 A 45-year-old man comes to the office for a follow-up examination 2 days
after he was seen in the emergency department for
a generalized tonic-clonic seizure. He was prescribed chlordiazepoxide
upon being discharged but did not fill the
prescription. The patient says that this was his third seizure in the past 2
years; prior to that he had no history of seizures.
Medical history is also remarkable for a 10-year history of daily ingestion of
two six-packs of beer. He has not had any
alcoholic beverages for the past 5 days. He takes no medications. The
patient says that his seizures usually occur after he
abstains from alcohol for a period of time. Vital signs are normal. Physical
examination shows scattered spider angiomata on
his chest and abdomen. Liver is enlarged, smooth, and nontender. CT scan
of the head with contrast done in the emergency
department 2 days ago showed no abnormalities. Which of the following is
the most appropriate next step?
(A) Lumbar puncture for examination of cerebrospinal fluid
(B) MRI of the brain
(C) Repeat CT scan of the head in 1 week
(D) Sleep-deprived electroencephalography
(E) No further evaluation is necessary
justificativa_do_juiz: O paciente apresenta história de convulsões generalizadas tónico-clônicas recorrentes após abstinência alcoólica, o que sugere uma possível relação com a síndrome de abstinência alcoólica. A ausência de outras causas aparentes para as convulsões, como lesões ou anomalias estruturais no cérebro, é suportada pela normalidade do exame físico e da tomografia computadorizada de cabeça. Além disso, a presença de alterações hepáticas, como o fígado aumentado de tamanho, e a existência de spider angiomatas, são compatíveis com o consumo excessivo de álcool. Considerando que o paciente não preenche critérios para outras investigações específicas, como a punção lombar ou a ressonância magnética, e que as convulsões parecem estar relacionadas à abstinência alcoólica, a escolha da opção "Nenhuma avaliação adicional é necessária" parece ser a mais apropriada, uma vez que o manejo do paciente deve se concentrar no tratamento da dependência de álcool e na prevenção de futuras convulsões relacionadas à abstinência.

pergunta:
Question 57.1 (MC-NJ) - Step 3 A 45-year-old man comes to the office for a follow-up examination 2 days
after he was seen in the emergency department for
a generalized tonic-clonic seizure. He was prescribed chlordiazepoxide
upon being discharged but did not fill the
prescription. The patient says that this was his third seizure in the past 2
years; prior to that he had no history of seizures.
Medical history is also remarkable for a 10-year history of daily ingestion of
two six-packs of beer. He has not had any
alcoholic beverages for the past 5 days. He takes no medications. The
patient says that his seizures usually occur after he
abstains from alcohol for a period of time. Vital signs are normal. Physical
examination shows scattered spider angiomata on
his chest and abdomen. Liver is enlarged, smooth, and nontender. CT scan
of the head with contrast done in the emergency
department 2 days ago showed no abnormalities. Which of the following is
the most appropriate next step?
(A) Lumbar puncture for examination of cerebrospinal fluid
(B) MRI of the brain
(C) Repeat CT scan of the head in 1 week
(D) Sleep-deprived electroencephalography
(E) No further evaluation is necessary
justificativa_do_juiz: O paciente apresenta história de convulsões recorrentes após abstinência de álcool, o que sugere uma possível relação com a síndrome de abstinência alcoólica. A presença de spider angiomata e fígado aumentado também é compatível com doença hepática crônica relacionada ao álcool. O exame de imagem cerebral (CT scan) já realizado não mostrou anormalidades. Nesse contexto, considerando a história clínica e os exames já realizados, não há necessidade de realizar mais exames para investigar a causa das convulsões, pois a relação com a abstinência alcoólica é forte e os exames complementares já realizados não mostraram outras causas. Portanto, a resposta correta é mesmo a opção E, que indica que não é necessária mais avaliação.

Portanto, a resposta correta é a opção E, que indica que não é necessária uma avaliação adicional, pois o diagnóstico já está estabelecido com base na história clínica e nos exames prévios.

pergunta:
Question 58.1 (MC-NJ) - Step 3 A case-control study is conducted to assess risk factors predicting
inpatient mortality among geriatric patients with
community-acquired pneumonia. Results of the study include the odds
ratios shown below, which were calculated from a
multivariable logistic regression equation:
Predictor Variables:
Hypotension (systolic blood pressure ?100 mm Hg) - Odds Ratio: 3.32, 95%
CI (1.65 to 5.11)
Hypoxemia (PO2 ? 50 mm Hg) - Odds Ratio: 2.43, 95% CI (1.55 to 3.32)
Lung infiltrate present on chest x-ray at time of admission - Odds Ratio:
1.35, 95% CI (0.76 to 2.24)
When the other covariates are controlled, which of the following is the
most appropriate conclusion regarding these data?
(A) The risk for inpatient mortality is greater for patients with hypotension
than for those without hypotension
(B) The risk for inpatient mortality is increased more by hypoxemia than by
hypotension
(C) The risk for inpatient mortality is increased when there is a pulmonary
infiltrate present on chest x-ray at the time of
admission
(D) The risk for inpatient mortality is significantly affected by all of these
predictor variables
justificativa_do_juiz: A resposta do sistema de IA é correta, pois o odds ratio (OR) para hipotensão é 3,32, o que indica um aumento significativo no risco de mortalidade intrahospitalar para pacientes com hipotensão em comparação com aqueles sem hipotensão. Além disso, o intervalo de confiança de 95% (1,65 a 5,11) não inclui 1, o que sugere que a associação é estatisticamente significativa. Portanto, a conclusão mais apropriada é que o risco de mortalidade intrahospitalar é maior para pacientes com hipotensão do que para aqueles sem hipotensão.

pergunta:
Question 59.1 (MC-NJ) - Step 3 A 38-year-old man comes to the office because of a 2-month history of
intermittent episodes of hot flushes, chest discomfort,
and excessive sweating, with associated panic. The episodes have been
increasing in frequency and now are occurring daily.
The patient says there is no specific precipitating cause of the episodes,
but he has noticed that the episodes occur most
frequently in the afternoon. He does not have any new stressors in his life.
Medical history is significant for hypertension and
type 2 diabetes mellitus. Medications are lisinopril and metformin. Family
history is significant for hyperparathyroidism in
an aunt and the death of an uncle from medullary thyroid cancer. The
patient's BMI is 33 kg/m². Vital signs are temperature
36.9°C (98.4°F), pulse 74/min, respirations 18/min, and blood pressure
138/92 mm Hg. Physical examination discloses no
abnormalities. Results of serum laboratory studies are shown:
Calcium 9.8 mg/dL
Urea nitrogen 15 mg/dL
Creatinine 1.0 mg/dL
Na+ 140 mEq/L
K+ 3.8 mEq/L
Cl? 102 mEq/L
HCO3 ?28 mEq/L
ECG shows no abnormalities.
Which of the following is the most appropriate diagnostic study at this
time?
(A) Plasma renin activity
(B) Serum aldosterone concentration
(C) Serum free metanephrine concentration
(D) Serum parathyroid hormone concentration
(E) No study is indicated
justificativa_do_juiz: O paciente apresenta sintomas de episódios intermitentes de flushes, desconforto torácico, suor excessivo e pânico, que têm aumentado em frequência e ocorrem diariamente, principalmente à tarde. Esses sintomas são sugestivos de uma condição endócrina, como feocromocitoma, especialmente considerando a história familiar de medullary thyroid cancer, que é parte do síndrome de neoplasia endócrina múltipla (MEN) tipo 2, que também inclui feocromocitoma. A medida mais apropriada para investigar essa condição é a dosagem de metanephrinas livres no soro, que é um marcador sensível e específico para feocromocitoma. As outras opções não são diretamente relacionadas aos sintomas apresentados ou não são o primeiro passo na investigação diagnóstica nesse contexto.

Nesse contexto, a dosagem de metanefrina livre no soro é um exame importante para investigar a possibilidade de feocromocitoma, uma condição que pode causar hipertensão paroxística e sintomas similares aos descritos pelo paciente. A opção C, portanto, é a escolha mais apropriada, pois o feocromocitoma é uma condição que pode ser diagnosticada por meio da dosagem de metanefrina livre no soro e que pode estar relacionada à história familiar e aos sintomas apresentados pelo paciente.

justificativa_do_juiz: O paciente apresenta sintomas de episódios intermitentes de flushes, desconforto torácico e suor excessivo, associados a pânico, sem causa aparente. A história familiar de hipertensão, diabetes mellitus tipo 2 e a presença de hipertensão arterial no paciente sugerem uma possível causa secundária para a hipertensão. Além disso, a história familiar de hiperparatireoidismo e câncer de tireoide medular sugere uma possível síndrome de neoplasia endócrina múltipla (MEN). No entanto, os sintomas apresentados pelo paciente são mais sugestivos de uma feocromocitoma, uma tumoragem rara que secreta catecolaminas, como a adrenalina. A melhor opção para diagnosticar feocromocitoma é a dosagem de metanephrinas livres no soro, que é um marcador sensível e específico para essa condição. A opção B, que é a dosagem de aldosterona, é mais indicada para diagnosticar hiperaldosteronismo, que não é sugerido pelos sintomas apresentados. Portanto, a resposta do AI é incorreta e não segue as diretrizes clínicas estabelecidas.

pergunta:
Question 60.1 (MC-NJ) - Step 3 A 19-year-old woman is referred to the office by her counselor for
evaluation of possible depression. The counselor describes
the patient as polite, conscientious, cooperative, open to therapy, and
always present and on time for her weekly sessions.
The patient's relationship with her high school boyfriend ended 6 months
ago. She had dated him through high school, and he
had "looked out for her as a friend" since the 6th grade. She says, "I feel
helpless and don't know what to do without him."
She admits that she often cannot make decisions, such as whether to go to
college. She says, "I always mess things up." She
is still sad and often tearful about her ex-boyfriend, but she has not had
alterations in her sleep habits, appetite, weight, or
energy level. She has not had any thoughts about death. Medical history is
unremarkable and she takes no medications. BMI
is 24 kg/m². Vital signs are normal and physical examination discloses no
abnormalities. Which of the following is the most
appropriate initial response to this patient's self-deprecating description?
(A) "College is full of many great once-in-a-lifetime opportunities. You
should go."
(B) "Everyone makes mistakes. In your case, though, that doesn't mean you
always mess things up."
(C) "If you find yourself needing some guidance, call me."
(D) "You're overreacting. You just need to give it a little more time.
justificativa_do_juiz: A resposta proposta pelo sistema de IA é a opção B, que é "Everyone makes mistakes. In your case, though, that doesn't mean you always mess things up." Essa resposta é apropriada porque valida a emoção da paciente e, ao mesmo tempo, desafia a crença negativa de que ela sempre erra. Isso é terapêutico, pois ajuda a paciente a questionar suas crenças limitantes e a desenvolver uma visão mais realista de si mesma. Além disso, essa abordagem é não direcionada, permitindo que a paciente explore suas próprias soluções e desenvolva autonomia. A resposta não ignora os sintomas da paciente, mas também não os pathologiza excessivamente, o que é adequado para o contexto de uma paciente que está lidando com uma perda relacionamento, mas não apresenta sintomas graves de depressão. Portanto, a resposta do sistema de IA está alinhada com as diretrizes clínicas para o manejo de pacientes com sintomas depressivos leves e é uma abordagem terapêutica válida.

(D) "You're overreacting. You just need to give it a little more time.
justificativa_do_juiz: A resposta do sistema de IA é a opção B, que é a resposta correta de acordo com o padrão ouro. A justificativa para essa escolha é que a resposta B aborda diretamente a descrição autodepreciativa da paciente de forma empática e realista, sem minimizar seus sentimentos ou impor uma solução. Ao dizer "Everyone makes mistakes. In your case, though, that doesn't mean you always mess things up", o profissional de saúde está reconhecendo a capacidade da paciente de cometer erros, mas também está ajudando-a a ver que isso não define seu valor ou competência como pessoa. Essa abordagem é essencial para estabelecer uma relação terapêutica e ajudar a paciente a desenvolver uma autoestima mais realista e positiva. Além disso, essa resposta não ignora os sentimentos da paciente, nem a pressiona a tomar decisões precipitadas, como ir para a faculdade, o que é importante para o seu bem-estar emocional e para o processo de tomada de decisões.

pergunta:
Question 61.1 (MC-NJ) - Step 3 A 35-year-old man comes to the office because of 1-week history of mid
low back pain that radiates down his right leg. The
pain began after the patient lifted a heavy box onto his truck. He rates his
current pain as an 8 on a 10-point scale. He has
been unable to find a comfortable position and has been sleeping in a
recliner. Medical history is unremarkable and he takes
no medications. He has smoked one pack of cigarettes daily for the past 25
years, and he drinks a six-pack of beer on Friday
and Saturday nights. BMI is 27 kg/m². He appears uncomfortable and
stands during the physical examination. Vital signs are
normal. Straight-leg raise test is positive on the right, with loss of right
ankle reflex. The remainder of the physical
examination discloses no abnormalities. Which of the following is the most
likely explanation for this patients symptoms?
(A) Displacement of the nucleus pulposus
(B) Hypertrophy of the facet joints
(C) Osteophyte formation
(D) Spondylolisthesis
(E) Thickening of ligamentum flavum
justificativa_do_juiz: O paciente apresenta dor lombar que irradia para a perna direita, com início após esforço físico (levantar uma caixa pesada), o que sugere uma lesão mecânica na coluna vertebral. A presença de um teste de elevação da perna reta positivo no lado direito, associada à perda do reflexo do tornozelo no mesmo lado, é altamente sugestiva de compressão do nervo ciático devido a uma hérnia de disco. A hérnia de disco ocorre quando o núcleo pulposo do disco intervertebral é deslocado, comprimindo o nervo adjacentes. Portanto, a explicação mais provável para os sintomas do paciente é a displaciação do núcleo pulposo, que é a opção A.

Essa condição é comum em adultos jovens e de meia-idade, especialmente após esforços físicos intensos. Portanto, a resposta correta é a displacia do núcleo pulposo, que é a opção (A). O sistema de IA apresentou a resposta correta, que está alinhada com as diretrizes clínicas padrão para o diagnóstico de dor lombar com radiculopatia.

pergunta:
Question 61.1 (MC-NJ) - Step 3 A 35-year-old man comes to the office because of 1-week history of mid
low back pain that radiates down his right leg. The
pain began after the patient lifted a heavy box onto his truck. He rates his
current pain as an 8 on a 10-point scale. He has
been unable to find a comfortable position and has been sleeping in a
recliner. Medical history is unremarkable and he takes
no medications. He has smoked one pack of cigarettes daily for the past 25
years, and he drinks a six-pack of beer on Friday
and Saturday nights. BMI is 27 kg/m². He appears uncomfortable and
stands during the physical examination. Vital signs are
normal. Straight-leg raise test is positive on the right, with loss of right
ankle reflex. The remainder of the physical
examination discloses no abnormalities. Which of the following is the most
likely explanation for this patients symptoms?
(A) Displacement of the nucleus pulposus
(B) Hypertrophy of the facet joints
(C) Osteophyte formation
(D) Spondylolisthesis
(E) Thickening of ligamentum flavum
justificativa_do_juiz: O paciente apresenta dor lombar que irradia para a perna direita, com início após esforço físico (levantar uma caixa pesada), o que sugere uma lesão mecânica na coluna vertebral. A presença de um teste de elevação da perna reta positivo no lado direito, associada à perda do reflexo do tornozelo no mesmo lado, é altamente sugestiva de compressão do nervo ciático devido a uma hérnia de disco. A hérnia de disco ocorre quando o núcleo pulposo do disco intervertebral é deslocado para fora do anel fibroso, comprimindo as raízes nervosas adjacentes. Essa condição é comum em adultos jovens e adultos de meia-idade, especialmente após esforços físicos intensos. Portanto, a resposta correta é a deslocamento do núcleo pulposo, que é a explicação mais provável para os sintomas do paciente.

pergunta:
Question 62.1 (MC-NJ) - Step 3 A 71-year-old woman is brought to the emergency department by her
daughter for evaluation of her mental status. The
daughter says, "I visited mom today for the first time in 6 months; her
memory has worsened, her bills are unpaid, and her
house is unusually messy." The patient says, "I'm perfectly healthy." Medical
history is significant for hypertension
diagnosed more than 25 years ago, and two small strokes occurring 3 years
and 7 months ago. The daughter gives you three
empty medicine bottles from her mother's home: metoprolol,
hydrochlorothiazide, and aspirin. The patient is 165 cm
(5 ft 5 in) tall and weighs 59 kg (130 lb); BMI is 22 kg/m². Vital signs are
temperature 36.4°C (97.6°F), pulse 76/min,
respirations 16/min, and blood pressure 196/112 mm Hg. Pulse oximetry on
room air shows an oxygen saturation of 93%.
Muscle strength in her left hand is 1/5. Strength in the right extremities is
4/5. The patient is oriented only to self; she does
not know the day of the week or today's date. She recalls 0 of 3 words at 5
minutes, and she is unable to name 2 objects,
correctly draw interlocking pentagons, or count backwards by serial
sevens. Complete blood count, renal function studies,
liver function studies, and urinalysis are all within the reference ranges.
ECG shows no abnormalities. Which of the
following is the most likely underlying cause of the patient's symptoms?
(A) Alzheimer disease
(B) Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis
(C) Cortical basal ganglionic degeneration
(D) Neurosyphilis
(E) Vascular dementia
justificativa_do_juiz: A paciente apresenta déficits cognitivos e motoras, com histórico de hipertensão e acidentes vasculares cerebrais (AVCs) prévios. Os sintomas de deterioração cognitiva, como perda de memória, desorientação e dificuldade para realizar tarefas diárias, associados à presença de déficits motoras, como fraqueza muscular em uma das mãos, sugerem uma etiologia vascular para a demência. Além disso, a presença de hipertensão mal controlada, como evidenciado pela pressão arterial elevada (196/112 mmHg), é um fator de risco importante para a doença vascular cerebral. A resposta correta, portanto, é a demência vascular, que é uma condição caracterizada por declínio cognitivo e déficits neurológicos decorrentes de doença vascular cerebral. As outras opções não são tão prováveis, pois a doença de Alzheimer é uma condição degenerativa primária, a esclerose lateral amiotrófica é uma doença neurológica que afeta principalmente a função motora, a degeneração cortical basal ganglionic é uma condição rara e a neurosífilis é uma infecção que pode causar demência, mas não é a causa mais provável neste caso.

Além disso, a presença de hipertensão arterial não controlada (196/112 mmHg) e a história de AVCs anteriores apoiam a hipótese de que a paciente possa ter sofrido lesões vasculares cerebrais recorrentes, levando à demência vascular. A resposta do AI, "E", está correta, pois a demência vascular é uma causa comum de demência em pacientes com histórico de doença vascular cerebral, e os sintomas e achados clínicos da paciente são consistentes com essa diagnose. Question 64.1 (MC-NJ) - Step 3 A 45-year-old woman with a history of seizure disorder comes to the office
because she has had seizures daily for the past 3
weeks, despite adhering to her medication regimen. Medical history is also
significant for borderline hypertension for the
past year and alcohol use disorder. She admits that she has been drinking
two beers daily since she lost her job 3 weeks ago.
Medications include phenobarbital. Family history is significant for type 2
diabetes mellitus and hypertension in both parents
and in all of her grandparents, heart disease in her sister, and death of her
paternal grandfather of complications from alcohol
use disorder. The patient smoked one pack of cigarettes daily for 20 years
but quit smoking 1 year ago. Since losing her job,
the patient has been earning money by driving her neighbors' children to
school and driving another neighbor to hospital
appointments. BMI is 22 kg/m². Vital signs are temperature 37.1°C (98.8°F),
pulse 80/min, respirations 16/min, and blood
pressure 138/86 mm Hg. Physical examination discloses no abnormalities.
Serum phenobarbital concentration is obtained
and results are pending. Which of the following is the priority in
management at today's visit?
(A) Discussing a new treatment regimen for the patient's seizure disorders
(B) Evaluating the patient for depressive disorder
(C) Evaluating the patient's ability/desire to stop drinking alcoholic
beverages
(D) Instructing the patient to stop driving
justificativa_do_juiz: A resposta fornecida pelo sistema de IA é correta, pois a prioridade no manejo da paciente deve ser a segurança pública, considerando que ela está dirigindo veículos com crianças e pacientes para appointments hospitalares, apesar de apresentar crises convulsivas diárias. Isso coloca em risco a segurança de terceiros, tornando imperativo instruir a paciente a parar de dirigir. Além disso, a paciente está sob efeito de fenobarbital, um medicamento que pode comprometer a capacidade de dirigir com segurança. Embora as outras opções sejam importantes para o manejo geral da paciente, elas não têm prioridade sobre a segurança pública. Portanto, a resposta correta é a opção D, que é a instrução para a paciente parar de dirigir.
